items.user_turn: I want you to apply those workshop techniques to my current appellate brief: produce line edits, a trimmed draft, and a short checklist of do/don't edits before filing. For context I’m working on a brief for client Jordan Alvarez, Case No.: 2024-CV-1123 — the working file is draft_brief_v3.docx. The court filing deadline is May 15, 2026 at 5:00 PM ET and my internal partner review is due May 10, 2026 at 12:00 PM ET. Privileged background: allegations of breach of contract and misrepresentation by Acme Corp, settlement strategy discussed in a privileged email dated November 3, 2025, and a key note to avoid attacking witness credibility of former CFO Maria Reyes because of a related pending matter. My target persuasive theme is contractual interpretation emphasizing ordinary meaning and the court’s formatting rules include the usual word limits and citation style — please produce concrete edits and a checklist aligned to these constraints.

items.user_turn: I want to finalize a revised client intake form and an implementation plan: updated question order and phrasing, consent language, and a two‑arm A/B test plan with success metrics and a stakeholder summary. For context I have the current intake files intake_sheet_v3.docx and intake_analytics_Q1_2026.csv; our client population includes Spanish-speaking survivors of domestic violence and recent immigrants who often fear sharing immigration status (so avoid collecting immigration status or home addresses when possible). Intake is handled by Jordan Reyes (junior associate), Mon–Thu 09:00–15:00 with a per-intake budget of $45 and contact phone 555-0234. Target KPIs are reducing intake time by 30% and raising completion to 90% — please produce a plan that respects the confidentiality and staffing constraints.
